clause.text:
CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT 

(Note 1: To be duly signed by a Key Managerial Personnel or a person duly authorised under a board 
resolution passed by the Bidder.) 

(Note 2: In case of the Consortium, this Agreement shall have entered into and submitted by each partner 
of the Consortium separately. The Agreement shall be duly signed by a Key Managerial Personnel of 
partner of the Consortium or person duly authorised under a board resolution passed by partner of the 
Consortium) 

This CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (hereinafter referred as the 
"Agreement") is made at […….] on this ___ day of _________, 20__ (hereinafter referred as “Effective 
Date”) between 

Coastal Energen Private Limited (CEPL), a company incorporated under the Companies Act, 1956 
and having its registered office at No. 5, Moores Road, Chennai, 600006, Tamil Nadu (hereinafter 
referred to as the “Company” or "Disclosing Party" which shall be deemed to mean and include its 
successors and assigns) of the FIRST PART 

And 

_____________________________, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, 1956 and having 
its Registered Office at ________________________ ____________________________ (hereinafter 
referred to as the “_________” or "Receiving Party" which shall be deemed to mean and include its 
successors and permitted assigns) of the SECOND PART; 

“Disclosing Party” and “Receiving Party” are individually referred to as “Party” and collectively referred 
to as “Parties”. 

WHEREAS: 

A. COASTAL ENERGEN PRIVATE LIMITED is engaged in the business of power generation. 

B. 

C. 

_____________________________ 
___________________________________________________________. 

engaged 

in 

is 

the 

business 

of 

SBI (Lead Bank) acting on behalf of the Lenders, proposes to invite proposals through a bidding 
process based on ‘Swiss Challenge Method’ and identify an investor for upfront full and final 
settlement of all outstanding in relation to the facilities availed by the Company from the Lenders 
(Transaction). 

D. With a view to enable the evaluation of the Transaction, CEPL will disclose certain confidential 

information (hereinafter defined) to the Receiving Party in respect of the Transaction. 

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration for the mutual promises and covenants hereunder the Disclosing 
Party and Receiving Party agree as follows:

classification.label: Confidentiality
classification.rationale_short: The clause is a confidentiality agreement between two parties, outlining the terms of confidential information disclosure.

1.2 “Representative” of a Party means any employee, agent, director, advisor, affiliate, investor, 

consultant, partner or any other person concerned or acting on their behalf.

classification.label: Definition of Confidential Info
classification.rationale_short: Defines a key term in the contract

why: This definition is standard and does not introduce significant risk

clause.text: 2. RECEIVING PARTY'S OBLIGATIONS

classification.label: Term & Survival
classification.rationale_short: The clause is about the receiving party's obligations, implying a term or condition in the contract.

analysis.plain_english_summary: The receiving party has obligations.

why: This clause is a standard term and survival clause, which is common in contracts and unlikely to cause significant business risk.

norm_deviation.suggested_revision: None

reasons: The clause is a standard term and survival clause, but the specific obligations may vary depending on the contract's context.

clause.text:
2.1 The Receiving Party agrees that the Confidential Information is to be considered confidential and 
proprietary to Disclosing Party, and Receiving Party shall hold the same in confidence. The 
Receiving Party and its Representative shall make use of the Confidential Information solely for the 
purpose of the Transaction or such other purposes from time to time agreed or consented by the 
Disclosing Party as evidenced in writing. The Receiving Party and any of its Representatives shall 
not without the prior consent of the Disclosing Party or as expressly permitted herein, disclose, 
publish or reveal or cause it to be disclosed, published or revealed or make available to any other 
person, or use or allow others to disclose or use, the Confidential Information in any manner 
whatsoever other than as provided in this Agreement.

classification.label: Confidentiality
classification.rationale_short: Clause defines confidentiality obligations for Receiving Party

flags.explanation: Clause may bind only one side; verify mutuality if intended.

analysis.plain_english_summary: The receiving party must keep confidential information confidential and only use it for the purpose of the transaction or with the disclosing party's consent.

why: The clause is standard and typical in confidentiality agreements, and the risk of non-compliance is relatively low.

norm_deviation.suggested_revision: none

reasons: The clause may not be comprehensive enough to cover all potential scenarios, but it is a good starting point.

clause.text:
2.2 The Receiving Party and its Representatives shall exercise no lesser security or degree of care than 
it applies to its own Confidential Information of an equivalent nature, but in any event not less than 
the degree of care which a reasonable person with knowledge of the confidential nature of the 
information would exercise.

classification.label: Confidentiality
classification.rationale_short: Clause specifies handling of confidential information

analysis.plain_english_summary: The receiving party must handle confidential information with the same level of care as they would their own similar information.

why: The receiving party is required to handle confidential information with a reasonable level of care, which is a standard industry practice.

norm_deviation.suggested_revision: None

reasons: The clause is a standard industry practice, but the specific level of care required may vary depending on the industry and jurisdiction.

clause.text:
2.3 Confidential Information furnished in tangible form shall not be duplicated or caused to be 
duplicated by Receiving Party or any of its Representatives. Upon the written request of Disclosing 
Party, Receiving Party shall return all Confidential Information received from the Disclosing Party 
in any form, including copies, or reproductions or other media containing such Confidential 
Information, within ten (10) days of such request. Any documents or other media developed by the 
Receiving Party containing Confidential Information shall be destroyed by Receiving Party. 
Receiving Party shall provide a written certificate to Disclosing Party regarding destruction within 
ten (10) days thereafter.

classification.label: Return/Destruction
classification.rationale_short: Clause specifies return and destruction of confidential information

analysis.plain_english_summary: The receiving party must return all confidential information and destroy any documents or media containing the information upon written request.

why: The risk of non-compliance is low as the request is written and the destruction is verified by a certificate.

comparison: industry standard for return and destruction of confidential information
norm_deviation.suggested_revision: None

reasons: The clause is clear and specific, but the effectiveness of the destruction process may depend on the implementation by the receiving party.

clause.text:
2.4 

It is agreed between the Parties that Receiving Party shall not disclose or direct its Representatives, 
to disclose (a) the Confidential Information has been made available to them, (b) that discussions or 
negotiations are taking place concerning a possible Transaction between the Parties or (c) any 
terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such possible Transaction, including the status 
thereof, to any third party without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party,

analysis.plain_english_summary: The Receiving Party must not disclose confidential information without the Disclosing Party's consent.

why: Disclosure of confidential information without consent may harm the Disclosing Party's business interests.

comparison: Typical confidentiality clauses often include a mutual obligation to keep information confidential.
norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider adding a reciprocal obligation for the Disclosing Party to maintain confidentiality.

reasons: The clause may only bind one side, and the intention to bind both parties is unclear.

clause.text:
EXCEPTIONS 

Confidential Information does not include any information that:

classification.label: Exceptions
classification.rationale_short: The clause is titled 'EXCEPTIONS'

analysis.plain_english_summary: Exceptions to Confidential Information

why: This clause defines what information is not considered Confidential Information, which may reduce the risk of misinterpretation and disputes.

norm_deviation.suggested_revision: None

reasons: The clause is clear and concise, but the definition of Confidential Information may vary depending on the context and jurisdiction.

clause.text:
3.1 

at the time of its disclosure, is in public domain or which after disclosure becomes part of public 
domain through no fault of the Receiving Party or its Representatives; or

classification.label: Exceptions
classification.rationale_short: Clause defines exceptions to confidentiality obligations

analysis.plain_english_summary: The Receiving Party is not liable for disclosing information that is already in the public domain or becomes public through no fault of the Receiving Party or its Representatives.

why: The Receiving Party is not responsible for disclosing information that is already public, so the risk is low.

comparison: This clause deviates from the standard exceptions clause, which typically does not include the 'public domain' exception.
norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider revising the clause to align with standard exceptions clauses.

reasons: The clause is unclear about what constitutes 'public domain' and how it applies to the Receiving Party's Representatives.

clause.text:
3.2 prior to its disclosure to the Receiving Party in connection with the Transaction was already in the 
possession of the Receiving Party or thereafter becomes known to the Receiving Party on a non-
confidential basis from a source other than the Disclosing Party.

classification.label: Exceptions
classification.rationale_short: Clause defines exceptions to confidentiality obligations

analysis.plain_english_summary: The receiving party already knew the information or learned it from a non-confidential source.

why: The information is already publicly known or was known by the receiving party before the transaction, so there is no risk of confidential information being disclosed.

norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider adding a specific time frame for when the receiving party can learn the information from a non-confidential source.

reasons: The clause is clear and specific, but the industry standard for non-disclosure agreements may vary depending on the jurisdiction and specific circumstances.

clause.text:
PERMITTED DISCLOSURES 

The Receiving Party shall restrict the possession, knowledge, development and use of the 
Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to its Representatives or any person claiming under it 

(a) who have a need to know such Confidential Information, and 

(b) who are bound by a non-disclosure agreement or confidentiality obligations consistent with 

and at least as protective as this Agreement. 

However, the Receiving Party will remain responsible for any breach of the terms of this 
Agreement by any of the persons mentioned herein to whom this Confidential Information would 
be disclosed by the Receiving Party.

classification.label: Permitted Disclosures
classification.rationale_short: Clause regulates disclosure of confidential information

analysis.plain_english_summary: The receiving party can only share confidential information with people who need to know it and are bound by a non-disclosure agreement or confidentiality obligations similar to this agreement.

why: The receiving party is responsible for any breaches by third parties, but the risk is relatively low as the third parties are bound by confidentiality obligations.

norm_deviation.suggested_revision: none

reasons: The clause is clear and specific, but the effectiveness of the confidentiality obligations depends on the receiving party's compliance and the third parties' adherence to their obligations.

clause.text:
5. COMPELLED DISCLOSURE 

The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to the extent necessary pursuant to 
applicable laws, regulation, court order, or other legal process, provided the Receiving Party 
notifies the Disclosing Party of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request 
so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive 
compliance with the provisions of this Agreement.

classification.label: Exceptions
classification.rationale_short: Allows disclosure under legal process

analysis.plain_english_summary: The receiving party can disclose confidential information if required by law, but must notify the disclosing party first.

why: The receiving party is required to disclose confidential information by law, and the disclosing party has the opportunity to seek a protective order or other remedy.

comparison: Typically, confidentiality agreements require the receiving party to seek permission from the disclosing party before disclosing confidential information.
norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider adding a provision that requires the receiving party to seek permission from the disclosing party before disclosing confidential information, unless required by law.

reasons: The clause may not be clear on the procedures for seeking a protective order or other remedy, and the disclosing party may not have sufficient time to respond to a legal request for disclosure.

clause.text:
6. NO LICENSE 

Nothing contained herein shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights by license or 
otherwise in any Confidential Information. It is understood and agreed that neither party solicits any 
change in the organization, business practice, service or products of the other party, and that the 
disclosure of Confidential Information shall not be construed as evidencing any intent by a party to 
purchase any products or services of the other party nor as an encouragement to expend funds in 
development or research efforts. Confidential Information may pertain to prospective or 
unannounced products. Receiving Party agrees not to use any Confidential Information as a basis 
upon which to develop or have a third party develop a competing or similar product.

classification.label: Confidentiality
classification.rationale_short: Grants no rights by license or otherwise in Confidential Information

analysis.plain_english_summary: The parties agree that nothing in this agreement grants any rights to confidential information and that the receiving party will not use this information to develop a competing product.

why: The clause is standard and typical in confidentiality agreements, and the parties are not likely to dispute its meaning.

norm_deviation.suggested_revision: none

reasons: The clause is clear and unambiguous, but there is a small chance that a party may interpret it differently in a specific context.

clause.text:
7. NO LIABILITY 

The Receiving Party understands and agrees that neither the Disclosing Party nor any of its 
directors, officers, employees, agents, advisors or representatives (i) have made or make any 
representation, warranty or guaranty, expressed or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of 
the Confidential Information or (ii) shall have any liability, responsibility or obligation to Receiving 
Party or its affiliates relating to or resulting from the use of the Confidential Information or any 
errors therein or omissions therefrom.

classification.label: Limitation of Liability
classification.rationale_short: Clause explicitly states no liability for Confidential Information

analysis.plain_english_summary: The receiving party agrees that the disclosing party has no liability for the accuracy or completeness of confidential information.

why: The limitation of liability is explicit and the receiving party has agreed to it.

comparison: Typically, liability for inaccurate or incomplete information is not explicitly limited.
norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider adding a specific time frame for the limitation of liability.

reasons: The clause is clear, but the suggested revision is subjective.

clause.text:
INJUNCTIVE RELIEF 

The Parties acknowledge that monetary damages alone may be an inadequate remedy for breach of 
the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement. In addition to any other remedy which may 
be available in law or equity, Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive order or other equitable 
relief to prevent a breach of this Agreement and to compel specific performance of this Agreement.

classification.label: Remedies / Injunctive Relief
classification.rationale_short: Injunctive relief mentioned explicitly

analysis.plain_english_summary: The disclosing party can ask for an injunction or other equitable relief if the receiving party breaches the agreement.

why: Injunctions can be costly and may disrupt business operations

comparison: Typically, injunctive relief is not explicitly mentioned in contracts
norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider adding a specific provision outlining the circumstances under which injunctive relief may be sought

reasons: The clause may be overly broad, and the parties may want to specify the circumstances under which injunctive relief is sought

clause.text:
3 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 This Agreement shall remain valid and binding on the Parties until one year from the Effective Date 
of this Agreement or signing of definitive agreement, whichever is earlier. The obligations under 
this Agreement shall be survive for a period of one (1) year from the expiry of this Agreement. 
Provided however that the obligation of the Receiving Party to keep the Confidential Information in 
confidence as set out in this Agreement shall survive the Term, including any early termination of 
this Agreement in accordance with the terms hereof.

classification.label: Term & Survival
classification.rationale_short: Survival of obligations after termination of the agreement

analysis.plain_english_summary: The agreement remains valid for 1 year from the effective date, and obligations survive for 1 year after expiration.

why: The agreement's duration is limited, and obligations are not overly burdensome.

comparison: Typically, agreements have longer durations and more extensive obligations
norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider revising the duration and obligations to better align with industry standards.

reasons: The agreement's duration and obligations may not be sufficient for all parties' needs, and the lack of specific details may lead to disputes.

clause.text:
10. GOVERNING LAW 

This Agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of India and the 
Parties consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts in Mumbai for any dispute arising out of 
this Agreement.

classification.label: Governing Law
classification.rationale_short: Clause explicitly states governing law and jurisdiction

analysis.plain_english_summary: The agreement will be governed by Indian law and any disputes will be resolved in Mumbai courts.

why: The governing law is specific to India, which may limit the jurisdiction of other courts.

norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider including a more neutral governing law clause to allow for flexibility in case of future disputes.

reasons: The clause does not specify the applicable law in case of conflicts between Indian and international law.
The clause may be subject to change due to future amendments to Indian law.

clause.text:
11.1 This Agreement constitutes the entire Agreement amongst the Parties with respect to the subject 
matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, representations, and 
understandings, whether written or oral, except obligations imposed by law which shall be deemed a 
part of this Agreement. This Agreement may not be amended except in writing signed by both Parties 
hereto. No failure or delay by any Party in exercising any right hereunder or any partial exercise 
thereof shall operate as a waiver thereof or preclude any other or further exercise of any right 
hereunder. The invalidity or unenforceability of any provision of this agreement shall not affect the 
validity or enforceability of any other provisions of this Agreement, which shall remain in full force 
and effect. Nothing in this Agreement shall be implied, except as required under statue.

classification.label: Term & Survival
classification.rationale_short: Refers to the duration and continuation of the agreement, including its supersession of prior agreements and the effect of invalid provisions.

analysis.plain_english_summary: This clause outlines the terms of the agreement and how it can be changed or terminated.

why: The clause does not introduce any significant risks to the business.

norm_deviation.suggested_revision: None

reasons: The clause is a standard term in many contracts, but its specific wording may vary depending on the jurisdiction.

clause.text:
11.2 The terms and conditions of this Agreement shall inure to the benefit of and be binding upon the 
successors and assigns of the Parties. The obligations under this Agreement shall not be assigned or 
otherwise transferred in whole or in part by Receiving Party without the prior written consent of the 
Disclosing Party. 

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written. 

By: _________________________ 
Name: 
Designation: 

[Company Name] 

By: _________________________ 
Name: 
Designation: 

[Company Name] 

4

classification.label: Term & Survival
classification.rationale_short: Survival of agreement terms and conditions

analysis.plain_english_summary: The terms and conditions of this agreement will benefit and bind the successors and assigns of the parties, and the receiving party cannot assign or transfer its obligations without the disclosing party's written consent.

why: The clause restricts the receiving party's ability to assign or transfer its obligations, which may limit its flexibility in certain situations.

comparison: Standard contract language typically allows for assignment or delegation of obligations with or without consent.
norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider revising to allow for assignment or delegation of obligations with or without consent, or to specify the circumstances under which consent is required.

reasons: The clause's enforceability may depend on the specific laws and regulations applicable to the parties and the agreement.
